messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following research institution participant consent form for GDPR compliance:

"This study is conducted by ResearchLab under ethical approval REF-2024-089. We will collect your age, gender, occupation, and responses to our questionnaire. Participation is voluntary and you may withdraw at any time without penalty. Withdrawing after data submission means your anonymised data may still be used in aggregate analysis. Data will be anonymised before analysis and retained for 10 years per research standards. Results will be published in anonymised form only. Contact research@researchlab.ac.uk for queries."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Consent is implied through voluntary participation but not explicitly named. Art. 6(1)(a) (consent) or Art. 6(1)(e) (public interest/scientific research) may apply — neither is stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Gender and questionnaire responses may include health, sexuality, or political belief data depending on study topic. Scientific research exemption (Art. 9(2)(j)) may apply but must be explicitly invoked.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Research study with aggregate analysis and anonymised publication is clearly stated.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Age, gender, occupation, and questionnaire responses are typical and proportionate for social research.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 10-year retention stated and grounded in research standards.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Withdrawal right stated but limited post-anonymisation. Rights to access, rectification, erasure, portability, restriction, and objection are not addressed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [FAIL] No mention of any processors (data analysis platforms, cloud storage).

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. Research data analysis platforms may involve non-EEA processing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not mentioned. Academic institutions processing research data at scale likely require a DPO.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not named), Art. 9 (special category risk not addressed, research exemption not invoked), Art. 15-21 (data subject rights incomplete), Art. 28 (processors not disclosed), Art. 46 (transfer information absent), Art. 37 (DPO likely required), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State explicit lawful basis (Art. 6(1)(a) consent or Art. 6(1)(e) public interest)
- If questionnaire touches on special category topics, invoke Art. 9(2)(j) scientific research exemption explicitly
- Add full data subject rights section including explanation of why erasure is limited post-anonymisation
- Disclose data analysis and storage platforms as processors
- Address international transfer safeguards if applicable
- Confirm DPO requirement for the institution
- Add breach notification commitment
